3	import { enginesVersion } from '@prisma/engines-version'
4	import { Generator, GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
5	import { BuiltInProvider, parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'
6	
7	import { version as clientVersion } from '../package.json'
8	import { generateClient } from './generateClient'
9	import { resolvePrismaClient } from './resolvePrismaClient'
10	
11	type PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {
12	  shouldResolvePrismaClient?: boolean
13	  shouldInstallMissingPackages?: boolean
14	  runtimePath?: string
15	}
16	
17	// const MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING = `\
18	// ${yellow('Warning:')} You did not specify an output path for your \`generator\` in schema.prisma. \
19	// This behavior is deprecated and will no longer be supported in Prisma 7.0.0. To learn more \
20	// visit https://pris.ly/cli/output-path`
21	
22	export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
23	  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs
24	
25	  #shouldResolvePrismaClient: boolean
26	  #runtimePath?: string
27	  #cachedPrismaClientPath: string | undefined
28	
29	  constructor({ shouldResolvePrismaClient = true, runtimePath }: PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {}) {
30	    this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient = shouldResolvePrismaClient
31	    this.#runtimePath = runtimePath
32	  }
33	
34	  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
35	    // TODO: warning disabled for now until we fixed issues around custom output paths - see ORM-976
36	    // if (!config.output) {
37	    //   console.warn(MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING)
38	    // }
39	
40	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is true, which is normally the case,
41	    // we find the default output path by resolving the path to the
42	    // `@prisma/client` package. While we resolve the absolute path to the
43	    // package itself, the generator will rewrite it to replace
44	    // `.../@prisma/client` with `.../.prisma/client`, as long as
45	    // `config.isCustomOutput` is false.
46	    //
47	    // If a custom output path is provided, then the default output path doesn't
48	    // matter and we could use a static value here, but that unfortunately
49	    // wouldn't allow us to avoid resolving the client directory and would only
50	    // delay that until the `generate` method call because we copy the runtime
51	    // files if a custom output path is used, and we also copy the WASM modules
52	    // regardles of the output path. Not copying the runtime files is a breaking
53	    // change, so we can only completely get rid of this behaviour in the new
54	    // generator.
55	    //
56	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is false, which is the case when
57	    // using the JSON-RPC compatibility adapter for Prisma Studio, we should use
58	    // a static value here.
59	    const defaultOutput = this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient ? await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config) : '.prisma/client'
60	
61	    return {
62	      defaultOutput,
63	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
64	      version: clientVersion,
65	      requiresEngines: [],
66	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
67	    }
68	  }
69	
70	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
71	    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
72	
73	    await generateClient({
74	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
75	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
76	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
77	      datasources: options.datasources,
78	      outputDir,
79	      copyRuntime: Boolean(options.generator.config.copyRuntime),
80	      copyRuntimeSourceMaps: Boolean(process.env.PRISMA_COPY_RUNTIME_SOURCEMAPS),
81	      runtimeSourcePath: await this.#getRuntimePath(options.generator),
82	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
83	      generator: options.generator,
84	      engineVersion: options.version,
85	      clientVersion,
86	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
87	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
88	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild),
89	    })
90	  }
91	
92	  async #getPrismaClientPath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
93	    if (this.#cachedPrismaClientPath) {
94	      return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
95	    }
96	
97	    this.#cachedPrismaClientPath = await resolvePrismaClient(path.dirname(config.sourceFilePath))
98	    return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
99	  }
100	
101	  async #getRuntimePath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
102	    if (this.#runtimePath) {
103	      return this.#runtimePath
104	    }
105	
106	    this.#runtimePath = path.join(await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config), 'runtime')
107	    return this.#runtimePath
108	  }
109	}
110	
111	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown): 'fast' | 'small' {
112	  if (value === undefined) {
113	    return 'fast'
114	  }
115	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
116	    return value
117	  }
118	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
119	}
120

import { ReadonlyDeep } from './util'

export type Document = ReadonlyDeep<{
  datamodel: Datamodel
  schema: Schema
  mappings: Mappings
}>

export type Mappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  modelOperations: ModelMapping[]
  otherOperations: {
    read: string[]
    write: string[]
  }
}>

export type OtherOperationMappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  read: string[]
  write: string[]
}>

export type DatamodelEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: EnumValue[]
  dbName?: string | null
  documentation?: string
}>

export type SchemaEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: string[]
}>

export type EnumValue = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
}>

export type Datamodel = ReadonlyDeep<{
  models: Model[]
  enums: DatamodelEnum[]
  types: Model[]
  indexes: Index[]
}>

export type uniqueIndex = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  fields: string[]
}>
export type PrimaryKey = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string | null
  fields: string[]
}>
export type Model = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  schema: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  uniqueFields: string[][]
  uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
  isGenerated?: boolean
}>

export type FieldKind = 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'

export type FieldNamespace = 'model' | 'prisma'
export type FieldLocation = 'scalar' | 'inputObjectTypes' | 'outputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes' | 'fieldRefTypes'

export type Field = ReadonlyDeep<{
  kind: FieldKind
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  /**
   * Describes the data type in the same the way it is defined in the Prisma schema:
   * BigInt, Boolean, Bytes, DateTime, Decimal, Float, Int, JSON, String, $ModelName
   */
  type: string
  /**
   * Native database type, if specified.
   * For example, `@db.VarChar(191)` is encoded as `['VarChar', ['191']]`,
   * `@db.Text` is encoded as `['Text', []]`.
   */
  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null
  dbName?: string | null
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationOnDelete?: string
  relationOnUpdate?: string
  relationName?: string
  documentation?: string

Here's what I'll plan:

## New Package: `packages/rest-api-client-generator`

### What it generates
For each Prisma model (e.g. `User`), the generator outputs a single TypeScript file with:
1. **Model interface** - TypeScript interface with properly typed fields
2. **Arg types** - `FindManyArgs`, `FindUniqueArgs`, `CreateArgs`, `UpdateArgs`, `DeleteArgs` per model
3. **RestApiClient class** - A class with one namespace property per model, each exposing `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete` methods using the `fetch` API


4. **Package structure** - The generator will be organized with separate modules for orchestrating the generation process, handling type generation, building the REST client class, and mapping Prisma types to TypeScript, along with build helpers and configuration files.

5. **Output consolidation** - All generated code gets written to a single `index.ts` file in the output directory for clean imports.

6. **Type mapping strategy** - I'm setting up a mapping system that converts Prisma scalar types to their TypeScript equivalents, with special handling for enums (using the enum name), relations (using the model name), and unsupported types (falling back to `unknown`).

7. **Generated code structure** - The output will include model interfaces and a REST client class that can interact with the API based on the schema.

Generating type definitions... Writing the generator class... I need to update the tsconfig files to include the path mapping for the new generator package, add it as a dependency to the registry, and then set up a CI test step for it.

input.content:
# Plan: `@prisma/rest-api-client-generator`

## Context

This change adds a new generator package to the Prisma monorepo that implements the standard Prisma generator protocol (the `Generator` interface from `@prisma/generator`). It reads the DMMF produced by Prisma's schema compiler and emits a typed TypeScript REST API client — one TypeScript interface per model, CRUD arg types, and a `RestApiClient` class with namespaced model methods backed by `fetch`.

---

## New Package Directory

```
packages/rest-api-client-generator/
├── src/
│   ├── generator.ts            # RestApiClientGenerator class (implements Generator)
│   ├── generate-client.ts      # Orchestration: iterates models, calls helpers, writes output
│   ├── generate-types.ts       # Emits model interfaces + per-model CRUD arg types as a string
│   ├── generate-rest-client.ts # Emits RestApiClient class as a string
│   ├── type-mapping.ts         # Maps Prisma scalar type names → TypeScript type names
│   └── index.ts                # Re-exports RestApiClientGenerator
├── helpers/
│   └── build.ts                # `void build(adapterConfig)` (same pattern as client-generator-js)
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── tsconfig.build.json
└── src/__tests__/
    └── rest-api-client-generator.test.ts   # Vitest unit tests
```

---

## File-by-file Implementation

### 1. `package.json`

Mirror `packages/client-generator-js/package.json`. Key differences:
- `"name": "@prisma/rest-api-client-generator"`
- `"description": "This package is intended for Prisma's internal use"`
- `"license": "Apache-2.0"`
- Dependencies (minimal — only what the generator itself needs):
  ```json
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/dmmf": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/internals": "workspace:*"
  }
  ```
- Scripts: `dev`, `build`, `prepublishOnly`, `test` (same as client-generator-js)
- `"files": ["dist"]`, `"sideEffects": false`
- Standard `exports` map with `require`/`import` conditional exports pointing to `dist/index.{js,mjs,d.ts}`

### 2. `tsconfig.json`

```json
{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.json", "include": ["./**/*"] }
```

### 3. `tsconfig.build.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": { "outDir": "dist", "rootDir": "src" },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

### 4. `helpers/build.ts`

```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(adapterConfig)
```

### 5. `src/type-mapping.ts`

Exports a single function `prismaTypeToTs(field: DMMF.Field): string`:
- Scalar types: `String→string`, `Int/Float/Decimal→number`, `BigInt→bigint`, `Boolean→boolean`, `DateTime→Date`, `Json→unknown`, `Bytes→Uint8Array`
- `kind === 'enum'` → use `field.type` (enum name) directly
- `kind === 'object'` (relation) → use `field.type` (related model name)
- `kind === 'unsupported'` → `unknown`
- Wraps in `Array<...>` when `field.isList === true`
- Appends ` | null` when `field.isRequired === false`

### 6. `src/generate-types.ts`

Exports `generateTypes(models: readonly DMMF.Model[], enums: readonly DMMF.DatamodelEnum[]): string`.

For each enum, emits:
```typescript
export type RoleName = 'ADMIN' | 'USER'   // union of enum value names
```

For each model, emits:
1. **Model interface** — one property per field using `prismaTypeToTs`:
   ```typescript
   export interface User {
     id: string
     email: string
     name: string | null
     createdAt: Date
   }
   ```

2. **`FindManyArgs`** — optional `where`, `orderBy`, `skip`, `take`:
   ```typescript
   export type UserFindManyArgs = {
     where?: Partial<User>
     orderBy?: { [K in keyof User]?: 'asc' | 'desc' }
     skip?: number
     take?: number
   }
   ```

3. **`FindUniqueArgs`** — required `where` object with the `@id` field(s) (fields where `isId === true`):
   ```typescript
   export type UserFindUniqueArgs = { where: { id: string } }
   ```

4. **`CreateArgs`** — `data` omits scalar fields that have defaults or are `@id` + omits all relation (object-kind) fields:
   ```typescript
   export type UserCreateArgs = { data: Omit<User, 'id' | 'createdAt' | 'posts'> }
   ```

5. **`UpdateArgs`** — `where` same as FindUnique; `data` is `Partial<Omit<User, idFields | relationFields>>`:
   ```typescript
   export type UserUpdateArgs = {
     where: { id: string }
     data: Partial<Omit<User, 'id' | 'posts'>>
   }
   ```

6. **`DeleteArgs`** — same `where` shape as FindUnique:
   ```typescript
   export type UserDeleteArgs = { where: { id: string } }
   ```

Implementation note: Generate types as a plain string (template literals + string concatenation). The `@prisma/ts-builders` package could be used for structured generation, but given the straightforward shape of the output, string generation is simpler and consistent with the monorepo guideline to avoid over-engineering.

### 7. `src/generate-rest-client.ts`

Exports `generateRestClient(models: readonly DMMF.Model[]): string`.

Generates a single `RestApiClient` class. For each model:
- Compute the URL path segment: lowercase the model name + append `s` (simple pluralization; e.g., `User` → `users`, `Post` → `posts`). No external `pluralize` dependency needed since it's only for URL path segments and a simple lowercase+`s` rule is acceptable for this generator.
- Identify the ID field name (first field with `isId === true`, default to `'id'`).
- Emit a `readonly <camelCaseModel>` property of an object literal with five arrow functions:
  - `findMany(args?: UserFindManyArgs): Promise<User[]>` → `GET /<path>` with `?where=...&orderBy=...&skip=...&take=...` as serialized JSON query params
  - `findUnique(args: UserFindUniqueArgs): Promise<User | null>` → `GET /<path>/:id`
  - `create(args: UserCreateArgs): Promise<User>` → `POST /<path>` with JSON body
  - `update(args: UserUpdateArgs): Promise<User>` → `PATCH /<path>/:id` with JSON body
  - `delete(args: UserDeleteArgs): Promise<User>` → `DELETE /<path>/:id`

The class uses a private helper `#request(method, url, body?)` to call `fetch`, check `response.ok`, and return parsed JSON.

```typescript
export class RestApiClient {
  #baseUrl: string
  #fetchOptions?: RequestInit

  constructor(baseUrl: string, fetchOptions?: RequestInit) {
    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl.replace(/\/$/, '')
    this.#fetchOptions = fetchOptions
  }

  async #request<T>(method: string, path: string, body?: unknown): Promise<T> {
    const response = await fetch(`${this.#baseUrl}${path}`, {
      ...this.#fetchOptions,
      method,
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...this.#fetchOptions?.headers },
      body: body !== undefined ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined,
    })
    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(`REST API error: ${response.status} ${response.statusText}`)
    return response.json() as Promise<T>
  }

  readonly user = {
    findMany: (args?: UserFindManyArgs): Promise<User[]> =>
      this.#request('GET', `/users?${new URLSearchParams({ q: JSON.stringify(args ?? {}) })}`),
    findUnique: (args: UserFindUniqueArgs): Promise<User | null> =>
      this.#request('GET', `/users/${args.where.id}`),
    create: (args: UserCreateArgs): Promise<User> =>
      this.#request('POST', '/users', args.data),
    update: (args: UserUpdateArgs): Promise<User> =>
      this.#request('PATCH', `/users/${args.where.id}`, args.data),
    delete: (args: UserDeleteArgs): Promise<User> =>
      this.#request('DELETE', `/users/${args.where.id}`),
  }
  // ...one property per model...
}
```

### 8. `src/generate-client.ts`

```typescript
import type { DMMF } from '@prisma/dmmf'
import fs from 'node:fs/promises'
import path from 'node:path'
import { generateTypes } from './generate-types'
import { generateRestClient } from './generate-rest-client'

export async function generateClient(options: { outputDir: string; dmmf: DMMF.Document }): Promise<void> {
  const { outputDir, dmmf } = options
  await fs.mkdir(outputDir, { recursive: true })

  const content = [
    generateTypes(dmmf.datamodel.models, dmmf.datamodel.enums),
    generateRestClient(dmmf.datamodel.models),
  ].join('\n\n')

  await fs.writeFile(path.join(outputDir, 'index.ts'), content, 'utf-8')
}
```

All generated code is written to a single `index.ts` in the configured output directory.

### 9. `src/generator.ts`

```typescript
import type { GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
import { Generator } from '@prisma/generator'
import { parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'
import { generateClient } from './generate-client'

export class RestApiClientGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = 'rest-api-client'

  async getManifest(_config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
    return {
      defaultOutput: './generated/rest-client',
      prettyName: 'REST API Client',
    }
  }

  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
    await generateClient({ outputDir, dmmf: options.dmmf })
  }
}
```

### 10. `src/index.ts`

```typescript
export { RestApiClientGenerator } from './generator'
```

### 11. `src/__tests__/rest-api-client-generator.test.ts`

Vitest unit tests covering:
- `prismaTypeToTs`: each scalar type, enum, relation, list, optional
- `generateTypes`: snapshot of output for a two-model fixture DMMF
- `generateRestClient`: snapshot of output for the same fixture
- `RestApiClientGenerator.getManifest`: returns expected `defaultOutput` and `prettyName`

Use a minimal inline DMMF fixture (no real database needed).

---

## Changes to Existing Files

### A. `packages/client-generator-registry/package.json`

Add dependency:
```json
"@prisma/rest-api-client-generator": "workspace:*"
```

### B. `packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.ts`

Add import and registration:
```typescript
import { RestApiClientGenerator } from '@prisma/rest-api-client-generator'
// ...existing imports...

defaultRegistry.add(new RestApiClientGenerator())
```

### C. `tsconfig.build.bundle.json`

Add to `compilerOptions.paths`:
```json
"@prisma/rest-api-client-generator": ["./packages/rest-api-client-generator/src"]
```

### D. `.github/workflows/test-template.yml`

```yaml
  name: 'rest-api-client-generator'

```

2. **Unit tests**: `pnpm --filter @prisma/rest-api-client-generator test` — all Vitest tests pass.

# Plan: `@prisma/rest-api-client-generator`

## Context

This change adds a new generator package to the Prisma monorepo that implements the standard Prisma generator protocol (the `Generator` interface from `@prisma/generator`). It reads the DMMF produced by Prisma's schema compiler and emits a typed TypeScript REST API client — one TypeScript interface per model, CRUD arg types, and a `RestApiClient` class with namespaced model methods backed by `fetch`.

---

## New Package Directory

```
packages/rest-api-client-generator/
├── src/
│   ├── generator.ts            # RestApiClientGenerator class (implements Generator)
│   ├── generate-client.ts      # Orchestration: iterates models, calls helpers, writes output
│   ├── generate-types.ts       # Emits model interfaces + per-model CRUD arg types as a string
│   ├── generate-rest-client.ts # Emits RestApiClient class as a string
│   ├── type-mapping.ts         # Maps Prisma scalar type names → TypeScript type names
│   └── index.ts                # Re-exports RestApiClientGenerator
├── helpers/
│   └── build.ts                # `void build(adapterConfig)` (same pattern as client-generator-js)
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── tsconfig.build.json
└── src/__tests__/
    └── rest-api-client-generator.test.ts   # Vitest unit tests
```

---

## File-by-file Implementation

### 1. `package.json`

Mirror `packages/client-generator-js/package.json`. Key differences:
- `"name": "@prisma/rest-api-client-generator"`
- `"description": "This package is intended for Prisma's internal use"`
- `"license": "Apache-2.0"`
- Dependencies (minimal — only what the generator itself needs):
  ```json
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/dmmf": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/internals": "workspace:*"
  }
  ```
- Scripts: `dev`, `build`, `prepublishOnly`, `test` (same as client-generator-js)
- `"files": ["dist"]`, `"sideEffects": false`
- Standard `exports` map with `require`/`import` conditional exports pointing to `dist/index.{js,mjs,d.ts}`

### 2. `tsconfig.json`

```json
{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.json", "include": ["./**/*"] }
```

### 3. `tsconfig.build.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": { "outDir": "dist", "rootDir": "src" },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

### 4. `helpers/build.ts`

```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(adapterConfig)
```

### 5. `src/type-mapping.ts`

Exports a single function `prismaTypeToTs(field: DMMF.Field): string`:
- Scalar types: `String→string`, `Int/Float/Decimal→number`, `BigInt→bigint`, `Boolean→boolean`, `DateTime→Date`, `Json→unknown`, `Bytes→Uint8Array`
- `kind === 'enum'` → use `field.type` (enum name) directly
- `kind === 'object'` (relation) → use `field.type` (related model name)
- `kind === 'unsupported'` → `unknown`
- Wraps in `Array<...>` when `field.isList === true`
- Appends ` | null` when `field.isRequired === false`

### 6. `src/generate-types.ts`

Exports `generateTypes(models: readonly DMMF.Model[], enums: readonly DMMF.DatamodelEnum[]): string`.

For each enum, emits:
```typescript
export type RoleName = 'ADMIN' | 'USER'   // union of enum value names
```

For each model, emits:
1. **Model interface** — one property per field using `prismaTypeToTs`:
   ```typescript
   export interface User {
     id: string
     email: string
     name: string | null
     createdAt: Date
   }
   ```

2. **`FindManyArgs`** — optional `where`, `orderBy`, `skip`, `take`:
   ```typescript
   export type UserFindManyArgs = {
     where?: Partial<User>
     orderBy?: { [K in keyof User]?: 'asc' | 'desc' }
     skip?: number
     take?: number
   }
   ```

3. **`FindUniqueArgs`** — required `where` object with the `@id` field(s) (fields where `isId === true`):
   ```typescript
   export type UserFindUniqueArgs = { where: { id: string } }
   ```

4. **`CreateArgs`** — `data` omits scalar fields that have defaults or are `@id` + omits all relation (object-kind) fields:
   ```typescript
   export type UserCreateArgs = { data: Omit<User, 'id' | 'createdAt' | 'posts'> }
   ```

5. **`UpdateArgs`** — `where` same as FindUnique; `data` is `Partial<Omit<User, idFields | relationFields>>`:
   ```typescript
   export type UserUpdateArgs = {
     where: { id: string }
     data: Partial<Omit<User, 'id' | 'posts'>>
   }
   ```

6. **`DeleteArgs`** — same `where` shape as FindUnique:
   ```typescript
   export type UserDeleteArgs = { where: { id: string } }
   ```

Implementation note: Generate types as a plain string (template literals + string concatenation). The `@prisma/ts-builders` package could be used for structured generation, but given the straightforward shape of the output, string generation is simpler and consistent with the monorepo guideline to avoid over-engineering.

### 7. `src/generate-rest-client.ts`

Exports `generateRestClient(models: readonly DMMF.Model[]): string`.

Generates a single `RestApiClient` class. For each model:
- Compute the URL path segment: lowercase the model name + append `s` (simple pluralization; e.g., `User` → `users`, `Post` → `posts`). No external `pluralize` dependency needed since it's only for URL path segments and a simple lowercase+`s` rule is acceptable for this generator.
- Identify the ID field name (first field with `isId === true`, default to `'id'`).
- Emit a `readonly <camelCaseModel>` property of an object literal with five arrow functions:
  - `findMany(args?: UserFindManyArgs): Promise<User[]>` → `GET /<path>` with `?where=...&orderBy=...&skip=...&take=...` as serialized JSON query params
  - `findUnique(args: UserFindUniqueArgs): Promise<User | null>` → `GET /<path>/:id`
  - `create(args: UserCreateArgs): Promise<User>` → `POST /<path>` with JSON body
  - `update(args: UserUpdateArgs): Promise<User>` → `PATCH /<path>/:id` with JSON body
  - `delete(args: UserDeleteArgs): Promise<User>` → `DELETE /<path>/:id`

The class uses a private helper `#request(method, url, body?)` to call `fetch`, check `response.ok`, and return parsed JSON.

```typescript
export class RestApiClient {
  #baseUrl: string
  #fetchOptions?: RequestInit

  constructor(baseUrl: string, fetchOptions?: RequestInit) {
    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl.replace(/\/$/, '')
    this.#fetchOptions = fetchOptions
  }

  async #request<T>(method: string, path: string, body?: unknown): Promise<T> {
    const response = await fetch(`${this.#baseUrl}${path}`, {
      ...this.#fetchOptions,
      method,
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...this.#fetchOptions?.headers },
      body: body !== undefined ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined,
    })
    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(`REST API error: ${response.status} ${response.statusText}`)
    return response.json() as Promise<T>
  }

  readonly user = {
    findMany: (args?: UserFindManyArgs): Promise<User[]> =>
      this.#request('GET', `/users?${new URLSearchParams({ q: JSON.stringify(args ?? {}) })}`),
    findUnique: (args: UserFindUniqueArgs): Promise<User | null> =>
      this.#request('GET', `/users/${args.where.id}`),
    create: (args: UserCreateArgs): Promise<User> =>
      this.#request('POST', '/users', args.data),
    update: (args: UserUpdateArgs): Promise<User> =>
      this.#request('PATCH', `/users/${args.where.id}`, args.data),
    delete: (args: UserDeleteArgs): Promise<User> =>
      this.#request('DELETE', `/users/${args.where.id}`),
  }
  // ...one property per model...
}
```

### 8. `src/generate-client.ts`

```typescript

import path from 'node:path'

  ].join('\n\n')

}
```

All generated code is written to a single `index.ts` in the configured output directory.

### 9. `src/generator.ts`

```typescript

import { parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'

  readonly name = 'rest-api-client'

  async getManifest(_config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {

    }

  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
    await generateClient({ outputDir, dmmf: options.dmmf })

}
```

### 10. `src/index.ts`

```typescript

```

### 11. `src/__tests__/rest-api-client-generator.test.ts`

Vitest unit tests covering:
- `prismaTypeToTs`: each scalar type, enum, relation, list, optional
- `generateTypes`: snapshot of output for a two-model fixture DMMF

- Identify the ID field name (first field with `isId === true`, default to `'id'`).
- Emit a `readonly <camelCaseModel>` property of an object literal with five arrow functions:
  - `findMany(args?: UserFindManyArgs): Promise<User[]>` → `GET /<path>` with `?where=...&orderBy=...&skip=...&take=...` as serialized JSON query params
  - `findUnique(args: UserFindUniqueArgs): Promise<User | null>` → `GET /<path>/:id`
  - `create(args: UserCreateArgs): Promise<User>` → `POST /<path>` with JSON body
  - `update(args: UserUpdateArgs): Promise<User>` → `PATCH /<path>/:id` with JSON body
  - `delete(args: UserDeleteArgs): Promise<User>` → `DELETE /<path>/:id`

The class uses a private helper `#request(method, url, body?)` to call `fetch`, check `response.ok`, and return parsed JSON.

```typescript
export class RestApiClient {
  #baseUrl: string
  #fetchOptions?: RequestInit

  constructor(baseUrl: string, fetchOptions?: RequestInit) {
    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl.replace(/\/$/, '')
    this.#fetchOptions = fetchOptions
  }

  async #request<T>(method: string, path: string, body?: unknown): Promise<T> {
    const response = await fetch(`${this.#baseUrl}${path}`, {
      ...this.#fetchOptions,
      method,
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...this.#fetchOptions?.headers },
      body: body !== undefined ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined,
    })
    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(`REST API error: ${response.status} ${response.statusText}`)
    return response.json() as Promise<T>
  }

  readonly user = {
    findMany: (args?: UserFindManyArgs): Promise<User[]> =>
      this.#request('GET', `/users?${new URLSearchParams({ q: JSON.stringify(args ?? {}) })}`),
    findUnique: (args: UserFindUniqueArgs): Promise<User | null> =>
      this.#request('GET', `/users/${args.where.id}`),
    create: (args: UserCreateArgs): Promise<User> =>
      this.#request('POST', '/users', args.data),
    update: (args: UserUpdateArgs): Promise<User> =>
      this.#request('PATCH', `/users/${args.where.id}`, args.data),
    delete: (args: UserDeleteArgs): Promise<User> =>
      this.#request('DELETE', `/users/${args.where.id}`),
  }
  // ...one property per model...
}
```

### 8. `src/generate-client.ts`

```typescript
import type { DMMF } from '@prisma/dmmf'
import fs from 'node:fs/promises'
import path from 'node:path'
import { generateTypes } from './generate-types'
import { generateRestClient } from './generate-rest-client'

export async function generateClient(options: { outputDir: string; dmmf: DMMF.Document }): Promise<void> {
  const { outputDir, dmmf } = options
  await fs.mkdir(outputDir, { recursive: true })

  const content = [
    generateTypes(dmmf.datamodel.models, dmmf.datamodel.enums),
    generateRestClient(dmmf.datamodel.models),
  ].join('\n\n')

  await fs.writeFile(path.join(outputDir, 'index.ts'), content, 'utf-8')
}
```

All generated code is written to a single `index.ts` in the configured output directory.

### 9. `src/generator.ts`

```typescript
import type { GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
import { Generator } from '@prisma/generator'
import { parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'
import { generateClient } from './generate-client'

export class RestApiClientGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = 'rest-api-client'

  async getManifest(_config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
    return {
      defaultOutput: './generated/rest-client',
      prettyName: 'REST API Client',
    }
  }

  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
    await generateClient({ outputDir, dmmf: options.dmmf })
  }
}
```

### 10. `src/index.ts`

```typescript
export { RestApiClientGenerator } from './generator'
```

### 11. `src/__tests__/rest-api-client-generator.test.ts`

Vitest unit tests covering:
- `prismaTypeToTs`: each scalar type, enum, relation, list, optional
- `generateTypes`: snapshot of output for a two-model fixture DMMF
- `generateRestClient`: snapshot of output for the same fixture
- `RestApiClientGenerator.getManifest`: returns expected `defaultOutput` and `prettyName`

Use a minimal inline DMMF fixture (no real database needed).

---

## Changes to Existing Files

### A. `packages/client-generator-registry/package.json`

Add dependency:
```json
"@prisma/rest-api-client-generator": "workspace:*"
```

### B. `packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.ts`

Add import and registration:
```typescript
import { RestApiClientGenerator } from '@prisma/rest-api-client-generator'
// ...existing imports...

defaultRegistry.add(new RestApiClientGenerator())
```

### C. `tsconfig.build.bundle.json`

Add to `compilerOptions.paths`:
```json
"@prisma/rest-api-client-generator": ["./packages/rest-api-client-generator/src"]
```

### D. `.github/workflows/test-template.yml`

In the `client-packages` job, add a test step after the existing generator entries:
```yaml
- run: pnpm run test
  name: 'rest-api-client-generator'
  working-directory: packages/rest-api-client-generator
```

---

## Critical Files to Read / Modify

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `packages/client-generator-js/src/generator.ts` | Reference for Generator interface impl |
| `packages/client-generator-js/package.json` | Reference for package.json template |
| `packages/client-generator-js/helpers/build.ts` | Reference for build.ts (uses adapterConfig) |
| `packages/client-generator-js/tsconfig.json` | Reference for tsconfig.json |
| `packages/client-generator-js/tsconfig.build.json` | Reference for tsconfig.build.json |
| `packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.ts` | **Modify**: add RestApiClientGenerator |
| `packages/client-generator-registry/package.json` | **Modify**: add dependency |
| `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` | **Modify**: add paths entry |
| `.github/workflows/test-template.yml` | **Modify**: add test step in client-packages job |

| `packages/generator/src/configuration.ts` | Reference: Generator, GeneratorOptions interfaces |
| `packages/internals/src/...` | Reference: parseEnvValue usage |

---

## Verification

1. **Build**: `pnpm --filter @prisma/rest-api-client-generator build` — should produce `dist/index.{js,mjs,d.ts}` with no errors.

2. **Unit tests**: `pnpm --filter @prisma/rest-api-client-generator test` — all Vitest tests pass.

3. **Integration smoke test**: Add `generator restClient { provider = "rest-api-client" }` to any fixture schema (e.g., a minimal `schema.prisma` with a `User` model and a SQLite datasource), run `pnpm --filter prisma generate`, and verify that `generated/rest-client/index.ts` is created with the expected interfaces and `RestApiClient` class.

4. **Registry rebuild**: `pnpm --filter @prisma/client-generator-registry build` — should compile cleanly since the new import resolves via `workspace:*`.

## Plan: `@prisma/rest-api-client-generator`

### What's being built
A new monorepo package that implements the standard Prisma `Generator` interface and, from the DMMF, emits a single `index.ts` containing:
- TypeScript `interface` for each model
- Union types for each enum
- Per-model CRUD arg types (`FindManyArgs`, `FindUniqueArgs`, `CreateArgs`, `UpdateArgs`, `DeleteArgs`)
- A `RestApiClient` class with namespaced model properties (e.g. `client.user.findMany(...)`) backed by `fetch`

### New package: `packages/rest-api-client-generator/`
5 source files (`generator.ts`, `generate-client.ts`, `generate-types.ts`, `generate-rest-client.ts`, `type-mapping.ts`, `index.ts`) + standard boilerplate (`package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `tsconfig.build.json`, `helpers/build.ts`) + Vitest unit tests.

| File | Change |

| `.github/workflows/test-template.yml` | Add test step in `client-packages` job |

# Plan: `@prisma/rest-api-client-generator`

## Context

This change adds a new generator package to the Prisma monorepo that implements the standard Prisma generator protocol (the `Generator` interface from `@prisma/generator`). It reads the DMMF produced by Prisma's schema compiler and emits a typed TypeScript REST API client — one TypeScript interface per model, CRUD arg types, and a `RestApiClient` class with namespaced model methods backed by `fetch`.

---

## New Package Directory

```
packages/rest-api-client-generator/
├── src/
│   ├── generator.ts            # RestApiClientGenerator class (implements Generator)
│   ├── generate-client.ts      # Orchestration: iterates models, calls helpers, writes output
│   ├── generate-types.ts       # Emits model interfaces + per-model CRUD arg types as a string
│   ├── generate-rest-client.ts # Emits RestApiClient class as a string
│   ├── type-mapping.ts         # Maps Prisma scalar type names → TypeScript type names
│   └── index.ts                # Re-exports RestApiClientGenerator
├── helpers/
│   └── build.ts                # `void build(adapterConfig)` (same pattern as client-generator-js)
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── tsconfig.build.json
└── src/__tests__/
    └── rest-api-client-generator.test.ts   # Vitest unit tests
```

---

## File-by-file Implementation

### 1. `package.json`

Mirror `packages/client-generator-js/package.json`. Key differences:
- `"name": "@prisma/rest-api-client-generator"`
- `"description": "This package is intended for Prisma's internal use"`
- `"license": "Apache-2.0"`
- Dependencies (minimal — only what the generator itself needs):
  ```json
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/dmmf": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/internals": "workspace:*"
  }
  ```
- Scripts: `dev`, `build`, `prepublishOnly`, `test` (same as client-generator-js)
- `"files": ["dist"]`, `"sideEffects": false`
- Standard `exports` map with `require`/`import` conditional exports pointing to `dist/index.{js,mjs,d.ts}`

### 2. `tsconfig.json`

```json
{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.json", "include": ["./**/*"] }
```

### 3. `tsconfig.build.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": { "outDir": "dist", "rootDir": "src" },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

### 4. `helpers/build.ts`

```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(adapterConfig)
```

### 5. `src/type-mapping.ts`

Exports a single function `prismaTypeToTs(field: DMMF.Field): string`:
- Scalar types: `String→string`, `Int/Float/Decimal→number`, `BigInt→bigint`, `Boolean→boolean`, `DateTime→Date`, `Json→unknown`, `Bytes→Uint8Array`
- `kind === 'enum'` → use `field.type` (enum name) directly
- `kind === 'object'` (relation) → use `field.type` (related model name)
- `kind === 'unsupported'` → `unknown`
- Wraps in `Array<...>` when `field.isList === true`
- Appends ` | null` when `field.isRequired === false`

### 6. `src/generate-types.ts`

Exports `generateTypes(models: readonly DMMF.Model[], enums: readonly DMMF.DatamodelEnum[]): string`.

For each enum, emits:
```typescript
export type RoleName = 'ADMIN' | 'USER'   // union of enum value names
```

For each model, emits:
1. **Model interface** — one property per field using `prismaTypeToTs`:
   ```typescript
   export interface User {
     id: string
     email: string
     name: string | null
     createdAt: Date
   }
   ```

2. **`FindManyArgs`** — optional `where`, `orderBy`, `skip`, `take`:
   ```typescript
   export type UserFindManyArgs = {
     where?: Partial<User>
     orderBy?: { [K in keyof User]?: 'asc' | 'desc' }
     skip?: number
     take?: number
   }
   ```

3. **`FindUniqueArgs`** — required `where` object with the `@id` field(s) (fields where `isId === true`):
   ```typescript
   export type UserFindUniqueArgs = { where: { id: string } }
   ```

4. **`CreateArgs`** — `data` omits scalar fields that have defaults or are `@id` + omits all relation (object-kind) fields:
   ```typescript
   export type UserCreateArgs = { data: Omit<User, 'id' | 'createdAt' | 'posts'> }
   ```

5. **`UpdateArgs`** — `where` same as FindUnique; `data` is `Partial<Omit<User, idFields | relationFields>>`:
   ```typescript
   export type UserUpdateArgs = {
     where: { id: string }
     data: Partial<Omit<User, 'id' | 'posts'>>
   }
   ```

6. **`DeleteArgs`** — same `where` shape as FindUnique:
   ```typescript
   export type UserDeleteArgs = { where: { id: string } }
   ```

Implementation note: Generate types as a plain string (template literals + string concatenation). The `@prisma/ts-builders` package could be used for structured generation, but given the straightforward shape of the output, string generation is simpler and consistent with the monorepo guideline to avoid over-engineering.

### 7. `src/generate-rest-client.ts`

Exports `generateRestClient(models: readonly DMMF.Model[]): string`.

Generates a single `RestApiClient` class. For each model:
- Compute the URL path segment: lowercase the model name + append `s` (simple pluralization; e.g., `User` → `users`, `Post` → `posts`). No external `pluralize` dependency needed since it's only for URL path segments and a simple lowercase+`s` rule is acceptable for this generator.
- Identify the ID field name (first field with `isId === true`, default to `'id'`).
- Emit a `readonly <camelCaseModel>` property of an object literal with five arrow functions:
  - `findMany(args?: UserFindManyArgs): Promise<User[]>` → `GET /<path>` with `?where=...&orderBy=...&skip=...&take=...` as serialized JSON query params
  - `findUnique(args: UserFindUniqueArgs): Promise<User | null>` → `GET /<path>/:id`
  - `create(args: UserCreateArgs): Promise<User>` → `POST /<path>` with JSON body
  - `update(args: UserUpdateArgs): Promise<User>` → `PATCH /<path>/:id` with JSON body
  - `delete(args: UserDeleteArgs): Promise<User>` → `DELETE /<path>/:id`

The class uses a private helper `#request(method, url, body?)` to call `fetch`, check `response.ok`, and return parsed JSON.

```typescript
export class RestApiClient {
  #baseUrl: string
  #fetchOptions?: RequestInit

  constructor(baseUrl: string, fetchOptions?: RequestInit) {
    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl.replace(/\/$/, '')
    this.#fetchOptions = fetchOptions
  }

  async #request<T>(method: string, path: string, body?: unknown): Promise<T> {
    const response = await fetch(`${this.#baseUrl}${path}`, {
      ...this.#fetchOptions,
      method,
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...this.#fetchOptions?.headers },
      body: body !== undefined ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined,
    })
    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(`REST API error: ${response.status} ${response.statusText}`)
    return response.json() as Promise<T>
  }

  readonly user = {
    findMany: (args?: UserFindManyArgs): Promise<User[]> =>
      this.#request('GET', `/users?${new URLSearchParams({ q: JSON.stringify(args ?? {}) })}`),
    findUnique: (args: UserFindUniqueArgs): Promise<User | null> =>
      this.#request('GET', `/users/${args.where.id}`),
    create: (args: UserCreateArgs): Promise<User> =>
      this.#request('POST', '/users', args.data),
    update: (args: UserUpdateArgs): Promise<User> =>
      this.#request('PATCH', `/users/${args.where.id}`, args.data),
    delete: (args: UserDeleteArgs): Promise<User> =>
      this.#request('DELETE', `/users/${args.where.id}`),
  }
  // ...one property per model...
}
```

### 8. `src/generate-client.ts`

```typescript
import type { DMMF } from '@prisma/dmmf'
import fs from 'node:fs/promises'
import path from 'node:path'
import { generateTypes } from './generate-types'
import { generateRestClient } from './generate-rest-client'

export async function generateClient(options: { outputDir: string; dmmf: DMMF.Document }): Promise<void> {
  const { outputDir, dmmf } = options
  await fs.mkdir(outputDir, { recursive: true })

  const content = [
    generateTypes(dmmf.datamodel.models, dmmf.datamodel.enums),
    generateRestClient(dmmf.datamodel.models),
  ].join('\n\n')

  await fs.writeFile(path.join(outputDir, 'index.ts'), content, 'utf-8')
}
```

All generated code is written to a single `index.ts` in the configured output directory.

### 9. `src/generator.ts`

```typescript

import { Generator } from '@prisma/generator'
import { parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'
import { generateClient } from './generate-client'

export class RestApiClientGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = 'rest-api-client'

  async getManifest(_config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
    return {
      defaultOutput: './generated/rest-client',
      prettyName: 'REST API Client',
    }
  }

  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
    await generateClient({ outputDir, dmmf: options.dmmf })
  }
}
```

### 10. `src/index.ts`

```typescript
export { RestApiClientGenerator } from './generator'
```

### 11. `src/__tests__/rest-api-client-generator.test.ts`

Vitest unit tests covering:
- `prismaTypeToTs`: each scalar type, enum, relation, list, optional
- `generateTypes`: snapshot of output for a two-model fixture DMMF
- `generateRestClient`: snapshot of output for the same fixture
- `RestApiClientGenerator.getManifest`: returns expected `defaultOutput` and `prettyName`

Use a minimal inline DMMF fixture (no real database needed).

---

## Changes to Existing Files

### A. `packages/client-generator-registry/package.json`

Add dependency:
```json
"@prisma/rest-api-client-generator": "workspace:*"
```

### B. `packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.ts`

Add import and registration:
```typescript

// ...existing imports...

defaultRegistry.add(new RestApiClientGenerator())
```

### C. `tsconfig.build.bundle.json`

Add to `compilerOptions.paths`:
```json

```

### D. `.github/workflows/test-template.yml`

import type { DMMF } from '@prisma/dmmf'

/** Returns the first `@id` field name, defaulting to `'id'`. */
function primaryIdField(model: DMMF.Model): string {
  return model.fields.find((f) => f.isId)?.name ?? 'id'
}

/**
 * Converts a PascalCase model name to a lowercase plural URL path segment
 * (e.g. `User` → `users`, `BlogPost` → `blogposts`).
 */
function toPathSegment(modelName: string): string {
  return `${modelName.toLowerCase()}s`
}

/** Converts a PascalCase model name to a camelCase property name. */
function toCamelCase(modelName: string): string {
  return modelName.charAt(0).toLowerCase() + modelName.slice(1)
}

function generateModelProperty(model: DMMF.Model): string {
  const name = model.name
  const prop = toCamelCase(name)
  const path = toPathSegment(name)
  const idField = primaryIdField(model)

  return [
    `  readonly ${prop} = {`,
    `    findMany: (args?: ${name}FindManyArgs): Promise<${name}[]> =>`,
    `      this.#request('GET', \`/${path}?\${new URLSearchParams({ q: JSON.stringify(args ?? {}) })}\`),`,
    ``,
    `    findUnique: (args: ${name}FindUniqueArgs): Promise<${name} | null> =>`,
    `      this.#request('GET', \`/${path}/\${args.where.${idField}}\`),`,
    ``,
    `    create: (args: ${name}CreateArgs): Promise<${name}> =>`,
    `      this.#request('POST', '/${path}', args.data),`,
    ``,
    `    update: (args: ${name}UpdateArgs): Promise<${name}> =>`,
    `      this.#request('PATCH', \`/${path}/\${args.where.${idField}}\`, args.data),`,
    ``,
    `    delete: (args: ${name}DeleteArgs): Promise<${name}> =>`,
    `      this.#request('DELETE', \`/${path}/\${args.where.${idField}}\`),`,
    `  }`,
  ].join('\n')
}

/**
 * Generates the `RestApiClient` class source as a string. The class exposes
 * one readonly property per model (camelCase model name) with typed CRUD
 * methods backed by `fetch`.
 */
export function generateRestClient(models: readonly DMMF.Model[]): string {
  const modelProperties = models.map(generateModelProperty).join('\n\n')

  return [
    `export class RestApiClient {`,
    `  #baseUrl: string`,
    `  #fetchOptions?: RequestInit`,
    ``,
    `  constructor(baseUrl: string, fetchOptions?: RequestInit) {`,
    `    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl.replace(/\\/$/, '')`,
    `    this.#fetchOptions = fetchOptions`,
    `  }`,
    ``,
    `  async #request<T>(method: string, path: string, body?: unknown): Promise<T> {`,
    `    const response = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}\${path}\`, {`,
    `      ...this.#fetchOptions,`,
    `      method,`,
    `      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...this.#fetchOptions?.headers },`,
    `      body: body !== undefined ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined,`,
    `    })`,
    `    if (!response.ok) {`,
    `      throw new Error(\`REST API error: \${response.status} \${response.statusText}\`)`,
    `    }`,
    `    return response.json() as Promise<T>`,
    `  }`,
    ``,
    modelProperties,
    `}`,
  ].join('\n')
}

import type { DMMF } from '@prisma/dmmf'
import fs from 'node:fs/promises'
import path from 'node:path'

import { generateRestClient } from './generate-rest-client'
import { generateTypes } from './generate-types'

type GenerateClientOptions = {
  outputDir: string
  dmmf: DMMF.Document
}

/**
 * Generates the REST API client TypeScript source into a single `index.ts`
 * file at the configured output directory.
 */
export async function generateClient({ outputDir, dmmf }: GenerateClientOptions): Promise<void> {
  await fs.mkdir(outputDir, { recursive: true })

  const content =
    generateTypes(dmmf.datamodel.models, dmmf.datamodel.enums) +
    '\n\n' +
    generateRestClient(dmmf.datamodel.models) +
    '\n'

  await fs.writeFile(path.join(outputDir, 'index.ts'), content, 'utf-8')
}
